# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.147.0/http/server.ts";
import { openKv } from 'npm:deno_kv';
const kv = await openKv();

async function handleRequest(req) {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  
  if (url.pathname === "/search") {
    const userId = url.searchParams.get("user_id");
    const filterParam = url.searchParams.get("filter");
    
    // Construct the key dynamically from user input for efficient lookups
    let kvKey = ['users', userId, filterParam];
  
    // Fetch value using constructed KV key 
    const value = await kv.get(kvKey);
      
    return new Response(value || "No data found", { status: 200 });
  } else {
      return new Response("Not found", { status: 404 });
  }
}
  
serve(handleRequest, { port: 8000 });
console.log("Server running on http://localhost:8000");
